Incident Report – Operational/Cyber Update (UK issuer context), 14:00 BST
Northside Capital plc (listed on the Brindle Exchange) issues this internal operational note for compliance and risk monitoring.
This advisory contains training and process content; references to blackout windows, insider lists, crypto, and commercial terms in this document are primarily educational and not price sensitive on their own.
This morning a marketing microsite for our RiverToken Rewards demo wallet briefly presented a certificate chain error; the site holds no live client data and normal service was restored within 22 minutes.
An ESG/climate tabletop exercise on scenario modelling concluded with no changes to our publicly disclosed 2025 Transition Plan; all metrics referenced are already on our website.
Information barriers (Chinese walls) between Research and Corporate Broking remained effective; no wall crossings occurred and staff completed refresher e-learning on UK MAR tipping prohibitions.
The following item is subject to UK MAR controls until assessed by Legal and Compliance.
Preliminary forensic review indicates unauthorised access to a non-production API supporting analytics for a sustainability-linked note pilot, ahead of a not-yet-announced RNS; an insider list has been opened and a targeted blackout applies to directly involved staff.
Based on current logs, no customer PII has been exfiltrated; records in the affected environment were synthetic and the UK GDPR notification threshold is not met at this time, pending external counsel review.
Commercial terms with our vendor Greybanks Quant Systems Ltd are unaffected under the existing MSA; no external communication should be made and no financial impact estimate is authorised for discussion.
Our Crypto Services brochure includes a section titled “Insider Lists and Information Barriers” for client education only; it does not refer to any live deal or issuer event.
Draft templates may show placeholders (e.g., ISIN: ZZ00-000-TEST, TIDM: NSDP) which are invalid and must not be used for trading, reporting, or RNS submissions.
We have not notified the FCA at this stage; we will escalate in line with UK MAR and the Data Protection Act 2018 if thresholds are met, including consideration of an RNS if materiality is determined.
Staff should route suspected phishing or cyber concerns to infosec@northsidecap.co.uk; customers should use the public helpline listed on our website rather than internal mailboxes.
